name: Oysters
recipeIngredient: Salt, 1/2 cup per gallon for washing meat
recipeInstructions:
1. Keep live oysters on ice until ready to can.
2. Wash shells thoroughly in cold water.
3. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
4. Place oysters on a tray and heat in the preheated oven for 5 to 7 minutes.
5. Remove from oven and let cool briefly in an ice bath.
6. Drain oysters, open shells, and remove meat.
7. Wash oyster meat in cold water with 1/2 cup salt per gallon.
8. Drain meat.
9. If desired, add 1/2 teaspoon salt to each pint of oyster meat.
10. Fill half-pint or pint jars with oyster meat and hot water, leaving 1-inch headspace.
11. Adjust jar lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1 or Table 2, based on the canning method used.
Table 1: Recommended process time for Oysters in a dial-gauge pressure canner.
Jar Size: Half-pints or Pints
Process Time: 75 minutes
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes:
0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
2,001 - 4,000 ft: 12 lb
4,001 - 6,000 ft: 13 lb
6,001 - 8,000 ft: 14 lb
Table 2: Recommended process time for Oysters in a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
Jar Size: Half-pints or Pints
Process Time: 75 minutes
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes:
0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb
Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb
